summary: Reviewed the supplied review pack for workflow and failure-path risks around the requested playlist-first workflow spec. The pack contains no diff, changed files, related files, or actual spec text, so the main risk is that the workflow cannot be validated against failure states, retries, or edge inputs from the provided evidence. Findings are limited to verifiability and specification gaps visible in the pack.
appears_sound: The project conventions identify the core integration boundaries that must be covered by this workflow: Spotify OAuth/API routes, Supabase live sessions, PDF/DOCX generation, and localStorage-to-Supabase sync.
The Definition of Done explicitly requires error-case tests, external service mocking, input validation, and full verification gates, which are the right controls for a multi-step playlist workflow.

findings.description: The two-phase playlist-first workflow cannot be reviewed because the pack contains no spec artifact, code diff, or related files. Failure sequence: reviewer approves the workflow from this pack, implementation proceeds, and failures between playlist selection, session creation, export, or live sync remain unexamined.
findings.evidence: The Review Pack states Changed Files '(none detected)', Diff '(no diff output)', Changed File Contents '(no files to include)', and Related Files '(no related files found by basename grep)'.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The spec or implementation may exist outside the pack but was not included in the reviewer input.
findings.what_would_confirm: Include the playlist-first workflow spec plus the affected host, session, Spotify, storage, and generation files or tests in a follow-up review pack.

findings.description: Intro song URL inputs are not specified, so invalid or unsupported URLs could pass early UI steps and fail later during Spotify calls, PDF generation, or live playback. Failure sequence: host enters an empty, malformed, non-Spotify, playlist, album, shortened, or region-restricted URL; the workflow accepts it; a later step cannot resolve the track and leaves the game partially prepared.
findings.evidence: The User Concerns ask whether intro song URL inputs are properly specified, but the pack provides no URL contract, validation rules, normalization behavior, or error states.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Existing unchanged code may already validate intro URLs, but it is not present in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Add the expected URL formats, normalization rules, rejection messages, and tests for invalid, missing, duplicate, and inaccessible intro song URLs.

findings.description: The two-phase workflow has no visible idempotency or retry contract. Failure sequence: host double-clicks or refreshes during playlist creation/session setup; the system may create duplicate playlists, duplicate sessions, or a session pointing at the wrong playlist unless a stable operation key or duplicate-handling rule exists.
findings.evidence: The pack describes Spotify playlist creation and Supabase live sessions in project conventions, but provides no new workflow spec covering double-submit, retry, refresh, or resume behavior.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The current app may already serialize host actions or deduplicate sessions in unchanged files.
findings.what_would_confirm: Specify idempotency keys or uniqueness rules for playlist creation and session creation, then include tests for double-submit and refresh during each phase.

findings.description: Partial completion handling is not defined for cross-service failures. Failure sequence: Spotify playlist creation succeeds, then Supabase session persistence or PDF generation fails; the host may see a failed setup while an orphan playlist or incomplete live session remains.
findings.evidence: The project conventions show the workflow crosses Spotify Web API, Supabase storage, and PDF/DOCX generation, but the pack includes no rollback, cleanup, resume, or user recovery rules.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Implementation may intentionally tolerate orphan playlists or provide manual cleanup outside the included pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Document the source of truth after each phase, cleanup expectations, and recovery UI for failures after each committed external operation.

empty_categories: No changed code was available to review for concrete race conditions, stale React state, or unmount cleanup defects.
No changed API handlers or server mutations were available to review for concrete timeout, retry, or transaction-boundary defects.
No UI implementation was available to review for empty-state crashes or navigation/back-button workflow regressions.
